name: green

metadata.name: green
metadata.summary: Green is a clean, colorful, fast python test runner.

documentation:
Green
=====

Green is a clean, colorful, fast python test runner.

Documentation
-------------

For full documentation please visit the `Github page for Green`_, or subscribe
to `Python Testing with Green`_ on Udemy.  You can find discounts for the Udemy
course in the documentation on Github.

Features
--------

- **Clean** - Low redundancy in output. Result statistics for each test is vertically aligned.
- **Colorful** - Terminal output makes good use of color when the terminal supports it.
- **Fast** - Tests run in independent processes.  (One per processor by default.  Does not play nicely with gevent.)
- **Powerful** - Multi-target + auto-discovery.
- **Traditional** - Use the normal ``unittest`` classes and methods for your unit tests.
- **Descriptive** - Multiple verbosity levels, from just dots to full docstring output.
- **Convenient** - Bash-completion and ZSH-completion of options and test targets.
- **Thorough** - Built-in integration with `coverage`_
- **Modern** - Supports Python 3.8+.  Additionally, `PyPy` is supported on a best-effort basis.
- **Portable** - macOS, Linux, and BSDs are fully supported.  Windows is supported on a best-effort basis.
- **Living** - This project grows and changes.  See the `changelog`_

.. _Github page for Green: https://github.com/CleanCut/green#green
.. _Python Testing with Green: https://www.udemy.com/python-testing-with-green/?couponCode=GREEN_ANGEL
.. _coverage: http://nedbatchelder.com/code/coverage/
.. _PyPy: http://pypy.org
.. _changelog: https://github.com/CleanCut/green/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

methods.name: green.test.test_process.TestPoolRunner.test_process
documentation:
Avoid FileNotFoundError when using a multiprocessing.Value, fixes #154.
        This test never fails, we have to watch the outer stderr to see if we get output like this:

  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.2_3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/multiprocessing/util.py", line 254, in _run_finalizers
    finalizer()
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.2_3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/multiprocessing/util.py", line 186, in __call__
    res = self._callback(*self._args, **self._kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.2_3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/shutil.py", line 465, in rmtree
    onerror(os.lstat, path, sys.exc_info())
  File "/usr/local/Cellar/python3/3.5.2_3/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/lib/python3.5/shutil.py", line 463, in rmtree
    orig_st = os.lstat(path)
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/var/folders/8y/cgqfhxyn2fz3r8n627_6dm_m0000gn/T/tmpp3fobx6i/pymp-8hpbali9'

        Newer versions of Python want to do their own cleanup, so let them.

methods.name: green.suite.GreenTestSuite.run
documentation: Emulate unittest's behavior, with Green-specific changes.

This version of a test suite has two important functions:

1) It brings Python 3.x-like features to Python 2.7
2) It adds Green-specific features  (see customize())

classes.name: green.output.Colors

methods.name: green.output.Colors.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Colors object.

Args:
    termcolor: If None, attempt to autodetect whether we are in a
        terminal and turn on terminal colors if we think we are.
        If True, force terminal colors on.
        If False, force terminal colors off.

methods.name: green.output.Colors.start_of_line
documentation: Movement

methods.name: green.output.Colors.bold
documentation: Real colors and styles

methods.name: green.output.Colors.passing
documentation: Abstracted colors and styles
A class to centralize wrapping strings in terminal colors.

classes.name: green.output.GreenStream

methods.name: green.output.GreenStream.writelines
documentation: Just for better compatibility with real file objects

methods.name: green.output.GreenStream.formatLine
documentation:
Takes a single line, optionally adds an indent and/or outcome
character to the beginning of the line.

methods.name: green.output.GreenStream.isatty
documentation: Wrap internal self.stream.isatty.

class_variables.name: green.output.GreenStream._ascii_only_output
documentation: default to printing output in unicode

Wraps a stream-like object with the following additional features:

1) A handy writeln() method (which calls write() under-the-hood)
2) Handy formatLine() and formatText() methods, which support indent
   levels, and outcome codes.
3) Compatibility with real file objects (by implementing real file object
   methods as we discover people need them).  So far we have implemented the
   following functions just for compatibility:
       writelines(lines)

modules.name: green.process
documentation: Handle running unittests suites in parallel.

functions.name: green.process.ddebug
documentation:
err can be an instance of sys.exc_info() -- which is the latest traceback
info
Super-useful debug function for finding problems in the subprocesses, and it
even works on windows

functions.name: green.process.rebuild_exc
documentation: Unmodified (see above)

functions.name: green.process.poolRunner
documentation:
I am the function that pool worker processes run.  I run one unit test.

coverage_config_file is a special option that is either a string specifying
the custom coverage config file or the special default value True (which
causes coverage to search for it's standard config files).
END of Worker Finalization Monkey Patching

variables.name: green.process.multiprocessing.pool.worker
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: green.process.ProcessLogger
documentation:
I am used by LoggingDaemonlessPool to get crash output out to the logger,
instead of having process crashes be silent.

classes.name: green.process.LoggingDaemonlessPool

methods.name: green.process.LoggingDaemonlessPool._repopulate_pool_static
documentation:
Bring the number of pool processes up to the specified number,
for use after reaping workers which have exited.

I make a pool of workers which can get crash output to the logger, run processes not as daemons,
and which run finalizers.

classes.name: green.process.RemoteTraceback
documentation: Unmodified (see above)

classes.name: green.process.ExceptionWithTraceback

modules.name: green.djangorunner
documentation:
To try running Django tests using green you can run:

    ./manage.py test --testrunner=green.djangorunner.DjangoRunner

To make the change permanent for your project, in settings.py add:

    TEST_RUNNER="green.djangorunner.DjangoRunner"

functions.name: green.djangorunner.django_missing
documentation: End of django fake config stuff

variables.name: green.djangorunner.DjangoRunner
documentation: type: ignore

methods.name: green.djangorunner.DjangoRunner.run_tests
documentation:
Run the unit tests for all the test labels in the provided list.

Test labels should be dotted Python paths to test modules, test
classes, or test methods.

A list of 'extra' tests may also be provided; these tests
will be added to the test suite.

Returns the number of tests that failed.
FIXME: extra_tests is not used, we should either use it or update the
documentation accordingly.

modules.name: green.cmdline
documentation: The green command line entry point.

modules.name: green.runner
documentation: Running tests.

functions.name: green.runner.run
documentation:
Run the given test case or test suite with the specified arguments.

Any args.stream passed in will be wrapped in a GreenStream

classes.name: green.runner.InitializerOrFinalizer
documentation:
I represent a command that will be run as either the initializer or the
finalizer for a worker process.  The only reason I'm a class instead of a
function is so that I can be instantiated at the creation time of the Pool
(with the user's customized command to run), but actually run at the
appropriate time.

modules.name: green.command
documentation: Registers the green command with setuptools.

functions.name: green.loader.toProtoTestList
documentation:
Take a test suite and turn it into a list of ProtoTests.

This function is recursive.  Pass it a suite, and it will re-call itself
with smaller parts of the suite.

functions.name: green.loader.toParallelTargets
documentation:
Produce a list of targets which should be tested in parallel.

For the most part, this will be a list of test modules.
The exception is when a dotted name representing something more granular
than a module was input (like an individual test case or test method).

This is green specific and not part of unittest/loader.py.

functions.name: green.loader.isPackage
documentation: Determine whether or not a given path is a (sub)package or not. Green specific.

functions.name: green.loader.findDottedModuleAndParentDir
documentation:
I return a tuple (dotted_module, parent_dir) where dotted_module is the
full dotted name of the module with respect to the package it is in, and
parent_dir is the absolute path to the parent directory of the package.

For filepath '/a/b/c/d.py' where b is the package, ('b.c.d', '/a')
would be returned.

This is green specific and not part of unittest/loader.py.

functions.name: green.loader.isTestCaseDisabled
documentation:
I check to see if a method on a TestCase has been disabled via nose's
convention for disabling a TestCase.  This makes it so that users can
mix nose's parameterized tests with green as a runner.

functions.name: green.loader.flattenTestSuite
documentation:
Look for a `doctest_modules` list and attempt to add doctest tests to the
suite of tests that we are about to flatten. Green specific.

classes.name: green.loader.GreenTestLoader

methods.name: green.loader.GreenTestLoader.discover
documentation:
I take a path to a directory and discover all the tests inside files
matching file_pattern.

If path is not a readable directory, I raise an ImportError.

If I don't find anything, I return None.  Otherwise I return a
GreenTestSuite
TODO: In unittest/loader.py this is not supposed to return None but it
always returns self.suiteClass(tests). Maybe we should do the same by
returning GreenTestSuite() but empty instead. It might be possible that
this is what is triggering the failures when running tests with the
@skipIf decorator with 3.12.1.

methods.name: green.loader.GreenTestLoader.loadTargets
documentation: Load the given test targets. This is green specific and not part of unittest.TestLoader.

methods.name: green.loader.GreenTestLoader.loadTarget
documentation: Load the given test target. This is green specific and not part of unittest.TestLoader.

This class is responsible for loading tests according to various criteria
and returning them wrapped in a GreenTestSuite. The super class wraps with
TestSuite.

modules.name: green.junit
documentation: Classes and methods to generate JUnit XML reports.

classes.name: green.junit.JUnitDialect
documentation: Hold the name of the elements defined in the JUnit XML schema (for JUnit 4).
TODO: consider using enum.Enum (new in py3.4) for the JUnitDialect and Verdict classes.

classes.name: green.junit.Verdict
documentation: Enumeration of possible test verdicts

classes.name: green.junit.JUnitXML

methods.name: green.junit.JUnitXML.save_as
documentation: Write the JUnit XML report to the given file-like object.

methods.name: green.junit.JUnitXML._convert_verdict
documentation: FIXME: test is not used.

Serialize a GreenTestResult object into a JUnit XML file, that can
be read by continuous integration servers, for example.

See GitHub Issue #104
See Option '-j' / '--junit-report'

modules.name: green.config
documentation: Handle the command line options and config file parsing.

functions.name: green.config.get_default_args
documentation: Set the defaults in a re-usable way.
TODO: switch to functools.cache after 3.9+ is the minimum supported version.

functions.name: green.config.parseArguments
documentation:
I parse arguments in sys.argv and return the args object.  The parser
itself is available as args.parser.

Adds the following members to args:
    parser    = the parser object
    store_opt = the StoreOpt object

functions.name: green.config.getConfig
documentation:
Get the Green config file settings.

All available config files are read.  If settings are in multiple configs,
the last value encountered wins.  Values specified on the command-line take
precedence over all config file settings.

Returns: A ConfigParser object.
Since this must be imported before coverage is started, we get erroneous
reports of not covering this function during our internal coverage tests.

functions.name: green.config.mergeConfig
documentation:
I take in a namespace created by the ArgumentParser in cmdline.main() and
merge in options from configuration files.  The config items only replace
argument items that are set to default value.

Returns: I return a new argparse.Namespace, adding members:
    shouldExit       = default False
    exitCode         = default 0
    include patterns = include-patterns setting converted to list.
    omit_patterns    = omit-patterns settings converted to list and
                       extended, taking clear-omit into account.
    cov              = coverage object default None
Since this must be imported before coverage is started, we get erroneous
reports of not covering this function during our internal coverage tests.

variables.name: green.config.coverage_version
documentation: pragma: no cover

variables.name: green.config.files_loaded
documentation: pragma: no cover

classes.name: green.config.StoreOpt
documentation:
Helper class for storing lists of the options themselves to hand out to the
shell completion scripts.

classes.name: green.config.ConfigFile
documentation:
Filehandle wrapper that adds a "[green]" section to the start of a config
file so that users don't actually have to manually add a [green] section.

modules.name: green.result
documentation: Classes and methods to handle test results.

functions.name: green.result.proto_test
documentation:
If test is a ProtoTest, I just return it.
Otherwise, I create a ProtoTest out of test and return it.

functions.name: green.result.proto_error
documentation:
If err is a ProtoError, I just return it.
Otherwise, I create a ProtoError out of err and return it.

classes.name: green.result.ProtoTest
documentation:
I take a full-fledged TestCase and preserve just the information we need
and can pass between processes.

classes.name: green.result.ProtoError
documentation:
I take a full-fledged test error and preserve just the information we need
and can pass between processes.

classes.name: green.result.BaseTestResult

methods.name: green.result.BaseTestResult.recordStdout
documentation:
Called with stdout that the suite decided to capture so we can report
the captured output somewhere.

methods.name: green.result.BaseTestResult.recordStderr
documentation:
Called with stderr that the suite decided to capture so we can report
the captured "errput" somewhere.

methods.name: green.result.BaseTestResult.displayStdout
documentation:
Displays AND REMOVES the output captured from a specific test.  The
removal is done so that this method can be called multiple times
without duplicating results output.

methods.name: green.result.BaseTestResult.displayStderr
documentation:
Displays AND REMOVES the errput captured from a specific test.  The
removal is done so that this method can be called multiple times
without duplicating results errput.

methods.name: green.result.BaseTestResult.addDuration
documentation:
Called when a test finished running, regardless of its outcome.

New in Python 3.12.

Args:
    test: The test case corresponding to the test method.
    elapsed: The time represented in seconds, including the
        execution of cleanup functions.
I am inherited by ProtoTestResult and GreenTestResult.

classes.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.__getstate__
documentation: Prevent the callback functions from getting pickled.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.__setstate__
documentation: Since the callback functions weren't pickled, we need to initialize them.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.startTest
documentation: Called before each test runs.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.stopTest
documentation: Called after each test runs.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.finalize
documentation:
I am here so that after the GreenTestSuite has had a chance to inject
the captured stdout/stderr back into me, I can relay that through to
the worker process's poolRunner who will send me back up to the parent
process.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addSuccess
documentation: Called when a test passed.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addError
documentation: Called when a test raises an exception.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addFailure
documentation: Called when a test fails a unittest assertion.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addSkip
documentation: Called when a test is skipped.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addExpectedFailure
documentation: Called when a test fails, and we expected the failure.

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addUnexpectedSuccess
documentation: Called when a test passed, but we expected a failure

methods.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.addSubTest
documentation:
Called at the end of a subtest no matter its result.

The test that runs the subtests calls the other test methods to
record its own result.  We use this method to record each subtest as a
separate test result.  It's very meta.
The _SubTest class is private and masked so we cannot easily type annotate.

class_variables.name: green.result.ProtoTestResult.failfast
documentation: Because unittest inspects the attribute
I'm the TestResult object for a single unit test run in a process.

classes.name: green.result.GreenTestResult

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.startTestRun
documentation: Called once before any tests run.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.stopTestRun
documentation: Called once after all tests have run.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.startTest
documentation: Called before the start of each test.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.stopTest
documentation: Supposed to be called after each test.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addSuccess
documentation: Called when a test passed.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addError
documentation: Called when a test raises an exception.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addFailure
documentation: Called when a test fails a unittest assertion.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addSkip
documentation: Called when a test is skipped.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addExpectedFailure
documentation: Called when a test fails, and we expected the failure.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.addUnexpectedSuccess
documentation: Called when a test passed, but we expected a failure.

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.printErrors
documentation:
Print a list of all tracebacks from errors and failures, as well as
captured stdout (even if the test passed, except with quiet_stdout
option).

methods.name: green.result.GreenTestResult.wasSuccessful
documentation: Tells whether or not the overall run was successful.
Aggregates test results and outputs them to a stream.
